title: erythronium americanum subsp. harperi
intro: Erythronium americanum subsp. harperi, also known as the Harper's yellow trout lily, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Liliaceae, characterized by its unique yellow flowers and strap-like leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Erythronium americanum subsp. harperi are believed to include the reduction of inflammation, improved wound healing, and the support of respiratory health. Erythronium americanum subsp. harperi can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine practices.
